In the *{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}* {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} compiled in {{ill|lt=Enchō (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Enchō|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}), the shrine is listed under {{ill|lt=Yamashiro Province|Q749276|WD=Q749276|1=Yamashiro Province}}, {{ill|lt=Sōraku District|Q1154661|WD=Q1154661|1=Sōraku District, Kyoto}} as “Okada Kamo Shrine, Major, Tsukinami and Niiname,” placing it among the Shikidai Taisha and noting that it received offerings at the court’s {{ill|lt=Tsukinami Festival|Q11516161|WD=Q11516161|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri}} and {{ill|lt=Niiname Festival|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}}. The shrine was originally located north of its present site (on the bank of the {{ill|lt=Kizugawa River (Kyoto)|Q3746236|WD=Q3746236|lt=Kizugawa River|1=Kizu River}}, where the site known as “Kamo Daimyōjin Ruins” remains). However, repeated flooding caused by changes in the river’s course led to its relocation to the current location. The present site is said to be the former location of the Okada Detached Palace of {{ill|lt=Empress Genmei|Q233224|WD=Q233224|1=Empress Genmei}}. After the palace fell into disuse, villagers established a shrine to preserve the former palace grounds, calling it “Tenjin Shrine.” Okada Kamo Shrine was later relocated into that precinct. Today, the Tenmangū stands as a subsidiary shrine within the grounds. === Precincts === File:Okada-Kamo-jinja, keidaisha-1.jpg|Subsidiary Kotohira Shrine (Kyoto Prefecture–designated cultural property) File:Okada-Kamo-jinja, keidaisha-2.jpg|Subsidiary Sanjūhassha-no-Kami (Kyoto Prefecture–designated cultural property: additional designation) File:Okada-Kamo-jinja-ato, sekihi.jpg|Stone monument marking the former site of Okada Kamo Shrine Yatagarasu at the purification pavilion of Okada Kamo Shrine.jpg|Purification pavilion of Okada Kamo Shrine === Cultural Properties === === Kyoto Prefecture–designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Properties ** Okada Kamo Shrine, 3 structures (buildings) – designated March 23, Heisei 30 (2018).From the Kyoto Prefectural Gazette dated March 23, 2018 (Notice No. 3 of the Kyoto Prefectural Board of Education). *** Main Hall and the Main Hall of the subsidiary Tenmangū, 2 structures (with 13 dedication plaques) *** Main Hall of the subsidiary Kotohira Shrine, 1 structure (with 3 dedication plaques) *** (Additional designation) Main Hall of the subsidiary Sanjūhassha-no-Kami, 1 structure *** (Additional designation) Two dedication plaques of the Main Hall of the subsidiary Sanjūhassha Shrine === Notes === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist}} === External Links === * {{Commonscat-inline|Okada-Kamo-jinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Okada Kamo Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Okada Kamo Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:木津川市の歴史]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:山城国の式内社|大おかたかもしんしや]] [[Category:京都府の旧郷社]] [[Category:春日移し]] [[Category:木津川市の建築物]] [[Category:賀茂県主氏|社おかたかもしんしや]] [[Category:pages]]

content == {{Shrine | Name = Okada Kamo Shrine | Image = [[File:Okada-Kamo-jinja, honden.jpg|280px]]
Main Hall (right) / Tenmangū (left)
(Kyoto Prefecture–designated cultural property) | Location = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kizugawa City|Q990352|WD=Q990352|1=Kizugawa, Kyoto}} Kamo-chō Kita 44 | Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Taketsunumi no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} | Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (Major)
Former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} | Founded = During the reign of the 10th {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}} | Main hall style = {{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} | Annual festival = {{ill|lt=April 3|Q2509|WD=Q2509|1=April 3}} | Map = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Okada Kamo Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Okada Kamo Shrine''' (おかだかもじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Kamo-chō Kita, {{ill|lt=Kizugawa City|Q990352|WD=Q990352|1=Kizugawa, Kyoto}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Shikidai Taisha|Q134917288|WD=Q134917288|1=Shikinai Taisha}}), and under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine rank|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. It enshrines {{ill|lt=Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto|Q11634939|WD=Q11634939|lt=Taketsunumi no Mikoto|1=Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto}}, the ancestral deity of the {{ill|lt=Kamo Agatanushi clan|Q2975287|WD=Q2975287|1=Kamo clan}}. === History === [[File:Okada-Kamo-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] According to a fragment of the *Yamashiro no Kuni Fudoki*, Taketsunumi no Mikoto traveled from Katsuragi in {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}, passed through Okada Kamo in Yamashiro Province, and came to rest at {{ill|lt=Kamo Mioya Shrine|Q701620|WD=Q701620|1=Shimogamo Shrine}} (Shimogamo Shrine) in northern Kyoto. It is said that during the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}}, the Kamo clan received a divided spirit from Shimogamo Shrine and founded this shrine at Okada Kamo, a place associated with Taketsunumi no Mikoto. The earliest documentary reference appears in the *{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}*, in an entry dated the 27th day of the first month of {{ill|lt=Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 1 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), which records that the shrine was granted the divine rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade ({{ill|lt=shinkai|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}}). In the *{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}* {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} compiled in {{ill|lt=Enchō (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Enchō|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}), the shrine is listed under {{ill|lt=Yamashiro Province|Q749276|WD=Q749276|1=Yamashiro Province}}, {{ill|lt=Sōraku District|Q1154661|WD=Q1154661|1=Sōraku District, Kyoto}} as “Okada Kamo Shrine, Major, Tsukinami and Niiname,” placing it among the Shikidai Taisha and noting that it received offerings at the court’s {{ill|lt=Tsukinami Festival|Q11516161|WD=Q11516161|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri}} and {{ill|lt=Niiname Festival|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}}. The shrine was originally located north of its present site (on the bank of the {{ill|lt=Kizugawa River (Kyoto)|Q3746236|WD=Q3746236|lt=Kizugawa River|1=Kizu River}}, where the site known as “Kamo Daimyōjin Ruins” remains). However, repeated flooding caused by changes in the river’s course led to its relocation to the current location. The present site is said to be the former location of the Okada Detached Palace of {{ill|lt=Empress Genmei|Q233224|WD=Q233224|1=Empress Genmei}}. After the palace fell into disuse, villagers established a shrine to preserve the former palace grounds, calling it “Tenjin Shrine.” Okada Kamo Shrine was later relocated into that precinct. Today, the Tenmangū stands as a subsidiary shrine within the grounds. === Precincts === File:Okada-Kamo-jinja, keidaisha-1.jpg|Subsidiary Kotohira Shrine (Kyoto Prefecture–designated cultural property) File:Okada-Kamo-jinja, keidaisha-2.jpg|Subsidiary Sanjūhassha-no-Kami (Kyoto Prefecture–designated cultural property: additional designation) File:Okada-Kamo-jinja-ato, sekihi.jpg|Stone monument marking the former site of Okada Kamo Shrine Yatagarasu at the purification pavilion of Okada Kamo Shrine.jpg|Purification pavilion of Okada Kamo Shrine === Cultural Properties === === Kyoto Prefecture–designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Properties ** Okada Kamo Shrine, 3 structures (buildings) – designated March 23, Heisei 30 (2018).From the Kyoto Prefectural Gazette dated March 23, 2018 (Notice No. 3 of the Kyoto Prefectural Board of Education). *** Main Hall and the Main Hall of the subsidiary Tenmangū, 2 structures (with 13 dedication plaques) *** Main Hall of the subsidiary Kotohira Shrine, 1 structure (with 3 dedication plaques) *** (Additional designation) Main Hall of the subsidiary Sanjūhassha-no-Kami, 1 structure *** (Additional designation) Two dedication plaques of the Main Hall of the subsidiary Sanjūhassha Shrine === Notes === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist}} === External Links === * {{Commonscat-inline|Okada-Kamo-jinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Okada Kamo Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Okada Kamo Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:木津川市の歴史]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:山城国の式内社|大おかたかもしんしや]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:春日移し]] [[Category:木津川市の建築物]] [[Category:賀茂県主氏|社おかたかもしんしや]] [[Category:pages]]

(Kyoto Prefecture–designated cultural property) | Location = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kizugawa City|Q990352|WD=Q990352|1=Kizugawa, Kyoto}} Kamo-chō Kita 44 | Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Taketsunumi no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} | Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (Major)
Former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} | Founded = During the reign of the 10th {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}} | Main hall style = {{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} | Annual festival = {{ill|lt=April 3|Q2509|WD=Q2509|1=April 3}} | Map = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Okada Kamo Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Okada Kamo Shrine''' (おかだかもじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Kamo-chō Kita, {{ill|lt=Kizugawa City|Q990352|WD=Q990352|1=Kizugawa, Kyoto}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Shikidai Taisha|Q134917288|WD=Q134917288|1=Shikinai Taisha}}), and under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine rank|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. It enshrines {{ill|lt=Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto|Q11634939|WD=Q11634939|lt=Taketsunumi no Mikoto|1=Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto}}, the ancestral deity of the {{ill|lt=Kamo Agatanushi clan|Q2975287|WD=Q2975287|1=Kamo clan}}. === History === [[File:Okada-Kamo-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] According to a fragment of the *Yamashiro no Kuni Fudoki*, Taketsunumi no Mikoto traveled from Katsuragi in {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}, passed through Okada Kamo in Yamashiro Province, and came to rest at {{ill|lt=Kamo Mioya Shrine|Q701620|WD=Q701620|1=Shimogamo Shrine}} (Shimogamo Shrine) in northern Kyoto. It is said that during the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}}, the Kamo clan received a divided spirit from Shimogamo Shrine and founded this shrine at Okada Kamo, a place associated with Taketsunumi no Mikoto. The earliest documentary reference appears in the *{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}*, in an entry dated the 27th day of the first month of {{ill|lt=Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 1 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), which records that the shrine was granted the divine rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade ({{ill|lt=shinkai|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}}). In the *{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}* {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} compiled in {{ill|lt=Enchō (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Enchō|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}), the shrine is listed under {{ill|lt=Yamashiro Province|Q749276|WD=Q749276|1=Yamashiro Province}}, {{ill|lt=Sōraku District|Q1154661|WD=Q1154661|1=Sōraku District, Kyoto}} as “Okada Kamo Shrine, Major, Tsukinami and Niiname,” placing it among the Shikidai Taisha and noting that it received offerings at the court’s {{ill|lt=Tsukinami Festival|Q11516161|WD=Q11516161|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri}} and {{ill|lt=Niiname Festival|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}}. The shrine was originally located north of its present site (on the bank of the {{ill|lt=Kizugawa River (Kyoto)|Q3746236|WD=Q3746236|lt=Kizugawa River|1=Kizu River}}, where the site known as “Kamo Daimyōjin Ruins” remains). However, repeated flooding caused by changes in the river’s course led to its relocation to the current location. The present site is said to be the former location of the Okada Detached Palace of {{ill|lt=Empress Genmei|Q233224|WD=Q233224|1=Empress Genmei}}. After the palace fell into disuse, villagers established a shrine to preserve the former palace grounds, calling it “Tenjin Shrine.” Okada Kamo Shrine was later relocated into that precinct. Today, the Tenmangū stands as a subsidiary shrine within the grounds. === Precincts === File:Okada-Kamo-jinja, keidaisha-1.jpg|Subsidiary Kotohira Shrine (Kyoto Prefecture–designated cultural property) File:Okada-Kamo-jinja, keidaisha-2.jpg|Subsidiary Sanjūhassha-no-Kami (Kyoto Prefecture–designated cultural property: additional designation) File:Okada-Kamo-jinja-ato, sekihi.jpg|Stone monument marking the former site of Okada Kamo Shrine Yatagarasu at the purification pavilion of Okada Kamo Shrine.jpg|Purification pavilion of Okada Kamo Shrine === Cultural Properties === === Kyoto Prefecture–designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Properties ** Okada Kamo Shrine, 3 structures (buildings) – designated March 23, Heisei 30 (2018).From the Kyoto Prefectural Gazette dated March 23, 2018 (Notice No. 3 of the Kyoto Prefectural Board of Education). *** Main Hall and the Main Hall of the subsidiary Tenmangū, 2 structures (with 13 dedication plaques) *** Main Hall of the subsidiary Kotohira Shrine, 1 structure (with 3 dedication plaques) *** (Additional designation) Main Hall of the subsidiary Sanjūhassha-no-Kami, 1 structure *** (Additional designation) Two dedication plaques of the Main Hall of the subsidiary Sanjūhassha Shrine === Notes === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist}} === External Links === * {{Commonscat-inline|Okada-Kamo-jinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Okada Kamo Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Okada Kamo Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:木津川市の歴史]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:春日移し]] [[Category:木津川市の建築物]] [[Category:賀茂県主氏|社おかたかもしんしや]] [[Category:pages]]

content == {{Shrine | Name = Okada Kamo Shrine | Image = [[File:Okada-Kamo-jinja, honden.jpg|280px]]
Main Hall (right) / Tenmangū (left)
(Kyoto Prefecture–designated cultural property) | Location = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kizugawa City|Q990352|WD=Q990352|1=Kizugawa, Kyoto}} Kamo-chō Kita 44 | Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Taketsunumi no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} | Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (Major)
Former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} | Founded = During the reign of the 10th {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}} | Main hall style = {{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} | Annual festival = {{ill|lt=April 3|Q2509|WD=Q2509|1=April 3}} | Map = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Okada Kamo Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Okada Kamo Shrine''' (おかだかもじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Kamo-chō Kita, {{ill|lt=Kizugawa City|Q990352|WD=Q990352|1=Kizugawa, Kyoto}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Shikidai Taisha|Q134917288|WD=Q134917288|1=Shikinai Taisha}}), and under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine rank|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. It enshrines {{ill|lt=Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto|Q11634939|WD=Q11634939|lt=Taketsunumi no Mikoto|1=Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto}}, the ancestral deity of the {{ill|lt=Kamo Agatanushi clan|Q2975287|WD=Q2975287|1=Kamo clan}}. === History === [[File:Okada-Kamo-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] According to a fragment of the *Yamashiro no Kuni Fudoki*, Taketsunumi no Mikoto traveled from Katsuragi in {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}, passed through Okada Kamo in Yamashiro Province, and came to rest at {{ill|lt=Kamo Mioya Shrine|Q701620|WD=Q701620|1=Shimogamo Shrine}} (Shimogamo Shrine) in northern Kyoto. It is said that during the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}}, the Kamo clan received a divided spirit from Shimogamo Shrine and founded this shrine at Okada Kamo, a place associated with Taketsunumi no Mikoto. The earliest documentary reference appears in the *{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}*, in an entry dated the 27th day of the first month of {{ill|lt=Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 1 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), which records that the shrine was granted the divine rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade ({{ill|lt=shinkai|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}}). In the *{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}* {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} compiled in {{ill|lt=Enchō (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Enchō|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}), the shrine is listed under {{ill|lt=Yamashiro Province|Q749276|WD=Q749276|1=Yamashiro Province}}, {{ill|lt=Sōraku District|Q1154661|WD=Q1154661|1=Sōraku District, Kyoto}} as “Okada Kamo Shrine, Major, Tsukinami and Niiname,” placing it among the Shikidai Taisha and noting that it received offerings at the court’s {{ill|lt=Tsukinami Festival|Q11516161|WD=Q11516161|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri}} and {{ill|lt=Niiname Festival|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}}. The shrine was originally located north of its present site (on the bank of the {{ill|lt=Kizugawa River (Kyoto)|Q3746236|WD=Q3746236|lt=Kizugawa River|1=Kizu River}}, where the site known as “Kamo Daimyōjin Ruins” remains). However, repeated flooding caused by changes in the river’s course led to its relocation to the current location. The present site is said to be the former location of the Okada Detached Palace of {{ill|lt=Empress Genmei|Q233224|WD=Q233224|1=Empress Genmei}}. After the palace fell into disuse, villagers established a shrine to preserve the former palace grounds, calling it “Tenjin Shrine.” Okada Kamo Shrine was later relocated into that precinct. Today, the Tenmangū stands as a subsidiary shrine within the grounds. === Precincts === File:Okada-Kamo-jinja, keidaisha-1.jpg|Subsidiary Kotohira Shrine (Kyoto Prefecture–designated cultural property) File:Okada-Kamo-jinja, keidaisha-2.jpg|Subsidiary Sanjūhassha-no-Kami (Kyoto Prefecture–designated cultural property: additional designation) File:Okada-Kamo-jinja-ato, sekihi.jpg|Stone monument marking the former site of Okada Kamo Shrine Yatagarasu at the purification pavilion of Okada Kamo Shrine.jpg|Purification pavilion of Okada Kamo Shrine === Cultural Properties === === Kyoto Prefecture–designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Properties ** Okada Kamo Shrine, 3 structures (buildings) – designated March 23, Heisei 30 (2018).From the Kyoto Prefectural Gazette dated March 23, 2018 (Notice No. 3 of the Kyoto Prefectural Board of Education). *** Main Hall and the Main Hall of the subsidiary Tenmangū, 2 structures (with 13 dedication plaques) *** Main Hall of the subsidiary Kotohira Shrine, 1 structure (with 3 dedication plaques) *** (Additional designation) Main Hall of the subsidiary Sanjūhassha-no-Kami, 1 structure *** (Additional designation) Two dedication plaques of the Main Hall of the subsidiary Sanjūhassha Shrine === Notes === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist}} === External Links === * {{Commonscat-inline|Okada-Kamo-jinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Okada Kamo Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Okada Kamo Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:木津川市の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Kasuga-Utsushi]] [[Category:木津川市の建築物]] [[Category:賀茂県主氏|社おかたかもしんしや]] [[Category:pages]]

After the palace fell into disuse, villagers established a shrine to preserve the former palace grounds, calling it “Tenjin Shrine.” Okada Kamo Shrine was later relocated into that precinct. Today, the Tenmangū stands as a subsidiary shrine within the grounds. === Precincts === File:Okada-Kamo-jinja, keidaisha-1.jpg|Subsidiary Kotohira Shrine (Kyoto Prefecture–designated cultural property) File:Okada-Kamo-jinja, keidaisha-2.jpg|Subsidiary Sanjūhassha-no-Kami (Kyoto Prefecture–designated cultural property: additional designation) File:Okada-Kamo-jinja-ato, sekihi.jpg|Stone monument marking the former site of Okada Kamo Shrine Yatagarasu at the purification pavilion of Okada Kamo Shrine.jpg|Purification pavilion of Okada Kamo Shrine === Cultural Properties === === Kyoto Prefecture–designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Properties ** Okada Kamo Shrine, 3 structures (buildings) – designated March 23, Heisei 30 (2018).From the Kyoto Prefectural Gazette dated March 23, 2018 (Notice No. 3 of the Kyoto Prefectural Board of Education). *** Main Hall and the Main Hall of the subsidiary Tenmangū, 2 structures (with 13 dedication plaques) *** Main Hall of the subsidiary Kotohira Shrine, 1 structure (with 3 dedication plaques) *** (Additional designation) Main Hall of the subsidiary Sanjūhassha-no-Kami, 1 structure *** (Additional designation) Two dedication plaques of the Main Hall of the subsidiary Sanjūhassha Shrine === Notes === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist}} === External Links === * {{Commonscat-inline|Okada-Kamo-jinja}} == Categories == content == {{Shrine | Name = Okada Kamo Shrine | Image = [[File:Okada-Kamo-jinja, honden.jpg|280px]]
Main Hall (right) / Tenmangū (left)
(Kyoto Prefecture–designated cultural property) | Location = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kizugawa City|Q990352|WD=Q990352|1=Kizugawa, Kyoto}} Kamo-chō Kita 44 | Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deity = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Taketsunumi no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} | Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (Major)
Former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} | Founded = During the reign of the 10th {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}} | Main hall style = {{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} | Annual festival = {{ill|lt=April 3|Q2509|WD=Q2509|1=April 3}} | Map = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Okada Kamo Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Okada Kamo Shrine''' (おかだかもじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Kamo-chō Kita, {{ill|lt=Kizugawa City|Q990352|WD=Q990352|1=Kizugawa, Kyoto}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Shikidai Taisha|Q134917288|WD=Q134917288|1=Shikinai Taisha}}), and under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine rank|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. It enshrines {{ill|lt=Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto|Q11634939|WD=Q11634939|lt=Taketsunumi no Mikoto|1=Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto}}, the ancestral deity of the {{ill|lt=Kamo Agatanushi clan|Q2975287|WD=Q2975287|1=Kamo clan}}. === History === [[File:Okada-Kamo-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] According to a fragment of the *Yamashiro no Kuni Fudoki*, Taketsunumi no Mikoto traveled from Katsuragi in {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}, passed through Okada Kamo in Yamashiro Province, and came to rest at {{ill|lt=Kamo Mioya Shrine|Q701620|WD=Q701620|1=Shimogamo Shrine}} (Shimogamo Shrine) in northern Kyoto. It is said that during the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}}, the Kamo clan received a divided spirit from Shimogamo Shrine and founded this shrine at Okada Kamo, a place associated with Taketsunumi no Mikoto. The earliest documentary reference appears in the *{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}*, in an entry dated the 27th day of the first month of {{ill|lt=Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 1 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), which records that the shrine was granted the divine rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade ({{ill|lt=shinkai|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}}). In the *{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}* {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} compiled in {{ill|lt=Enchō (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Enchō|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}), the shrine is listed under {{ill|lt=Yamashiro Province|Q749276|WD=Q749276|1=Yamashiro Province}}, {{ill|lt=Sōraku District|Q1154661|WD=Q1154661|1=Sōraku District, Kyoto}} as “Okada Kamo Shrine, Major, Tsukinami and Niiname,” placing it among the Shikidai Taisha and noting that it received offerings at the court’s {{ill|lt=Tsukinami Festival|Q11516161|WD=Q11516161|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri}} and {{ill|lt=Niiname Festival|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}}. The shrine was originally located north of its present site (on the bank of the {{ill|lt=Kizugawa River (Kyoto)|Q3746236|WD=Q3746236|lt=Kizugawa River|1=Kizu River}}, where the site known as “Kamo Daimyōjin Ruins” remains). However, repeated flooding caused by changes in the river’s course led to its relocation to the current location. The present site is said to be the former location of the Okada Detached Palace of {{ill|lt=Empress Genmei|Q233224|WD=Q233224|1=Empress Genmei}}. After the palace fell into disuse, villagers established a shrine to preserve the former palace grounds, calling it “Tenjin Shrine.” Okada Kamo Shrine was later relocated into that precinct. Today, the Tenmangū stands as a subsidiary shrine within the grounds. === Precincts === File:Okada-Kamo-jinja, keidaisha-1.jpg|Subsidiary Kotohira Shrine (Kyoto Prefecture–designated cultural property) File:Okada-Kamo-jinja, keidaisha-2.jpg|Subsidiary Sanjūhassha-no-Kami (Kyoto Prefecture–designated cultural property: additional designation) File:Okada-Kamo-jinja-ato, sekihi.jpg|Stone monument marking the former site of Okada Kamo Shrine Yatagarasu at the purification pavilion of Okada Kamo Shrine.jpg|Purification pavilion of Okada Kamo Shrine === Cultural Properties === === Kyoto Prefecture–designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Properties ** Okada Kamo Shrine, 3 structures (buildings) – designated March 23, Heisei 30 (2018).From the Kyoto Prefectural Gazette dated March 23, 2018 (Notice No. 3 of the Kyoto Prefectural Board of Education). *** Main Hall and the Main Hall of the subsidiary Tenmangū, 2 structures (with 13 dedication plaques) *** Main Hall of the subsidiary Kotohira Shrine, 1 structure (with 3 dedication plaques) *** (Additional designation) Main Hall of the subsidiary Sanjūhassha-no-Kami, 1 structure *** (Additional designation) Two dedication plaques of the Main Hall of the subsidiary Sanjūhassha Shrine === Notes === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist}} === External Links === * {{Commonscat-inline|Okada-Kamo-jinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Okada Kamo Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Okada Kamo Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kizugawa, Kyoto]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Kasuga-Utsushi]] [[Category:木津川市の建築物]] [[Category:Kamo clan]] [[Category:pages]]

Former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} | Founded = During the reign of the 10th {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}} | Main hall style = {{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} | Annual festival = {{ill|lt=April 3|Q2509|WD=Q2509|1=April 3}} | Map = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Okada Kamo Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Okada Kamo Shrine''' (おかだかもじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Kamo-chō Kita, {{ill|lt=Kizugawa City|Q990352|WD=Q990352|1=Kizugawa, Kyoto}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Shikidai Taisha|Q134917288|WD=Q134917288|1=Shikinai Taisha}}), and under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine rank|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. It enshrines {{ill|lt=Kamo Taketsunumi no Mikoto|Q11634939|WD=Q11634939|lt=Taketsunumi no Mikoto|1=Kamotaketsunumi no Mikoto}}, the ancestral deity of the {{ill|lt=Kamo Agatanushi clan|Q2975287|WD=Q2975287|1=Kamo clan}}. === History === [[File:Okada-Kamo-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] According to a fragment of the *Yamashiro no Kuni Fudoki*, Taketsunumi no Mikoto traveled from Katsuragi in {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}, passed through Okada Kamo in Yamashiro Province, and came to rest at {{ill|lt=Kamo Mioya Shrine|Q701620|WD=Q701620|1=Shimogamo Shrine}} (Shimogamo Shrine) in northern Kyoto. It is said that during the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}}, the Kamo clan received a divided spirit from Shimogamo Shrine and founded this shrine at Okada Kamo, a place associated with Taketsunumi no Mikoto. The earliest documentary reference appears in the *{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}*, in an entry dated the 27th day of the first month of {{ill|lt=Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 1 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), which records that the shrine was granted the divine rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade ({{ill|lt=shinkai|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}}). In the *{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}* {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} compiled in {{ill|lt=Enchō (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Enchō|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}), the shrine is listed under {{ill|lt=Yamashiro Province|Q749276|WD=Q749276|1=Yamashiro Province}}, {{ill|lt=Sōraku District|Q1154661|WD=Q1154661|1=Sōraku District, Kyoto}} as “Okada Kamo Shrine, Major, Tsukinami and Niiname,” placing it among the Shikidai Taisha and noting that it received offerings at the court’s {{ill|lt=Tsukinami Festival|Q11516161|WD=Q11516161|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri}} and {{ill|lt=Niiname Festival|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}}. The shrine was originally located north of its present site (on the bank of the {{ill|lt=Kizugawa River (Kyoto)|Q3746236|WD=Q3746236|lt=Kizugawa River|1=Kizu River}}, where the site known as “Kamo Daimyōjin Ruins” remains). However, repeated flooding caused by changes in the river’s course led to its relocation to the current location. The present site is said to be the former location of the Okada Detached Palace of {{ill|lt=Empress Genmei|Q233224|WD=Q233224|1=Empress Genmei}}. After the palace fell into disuse, villagers established a shrine to preserve the former palace grounds, calling it “Tenjin Shrine.” Okada Kamo Shrine was later relocated into that precinct. Today, the Tenmangū stands as a subsidiary shrine within the grounds. === Precincts === File:Okada-Kamo-jinja, keidaisha-1.jpg|Subsidiary Kotohira Shrine (Kyoto Prefecture–designated cultural property) File:Okada-Kamo-jinja, keidaisha-2.jpg|Subsidiary Sanjūhassha-no-Kami (Kyoto Prefecture–designated cultural property: additional designation) File:Okada-Kamo-jinja-ato, sekihi.jpg|Stone monument marking the former site of Okada Kamo Shrine Yatagarasu at the purification pavilion of Okada Kamo Shrine.jpg|Purification pavilion of Okada Kamo Shrine === Cultural Properties === === Kyoto Prefecture–designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Properties ** Okada Kamo Shrine, 3 structures (buildings) – designated March 23, Heisei 30 (2018).From the Kyoto Prefectural Gazette dated March 23, 2018 (Notice No. 3 of the Kyoto Prefectural Board of Education). *** Main Hall and the Main Hall of the subsidiary Tenmangū, 2 structures (with 13 dedication plaques) *** Main Hall of the subsidiary Kotohira Shrine, 1 structure (with 3 dedication plaques) *** (Additional designation) Main Hall of the subsidiary Sanjūhassha-no-Kami, 1 structure *** (Additional designation) Two dedication plaques of the Main Hall of the subsidiary Sanjūhassha Shrine === Notes === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist}} === External Links === * {{Commonscat-inline|Okada-Kamo-jinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Okada Kamo Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Okada Kamo Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kizugawa, Kyoto]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Kasuga-Utsushi]] [[Category:木津川市の建築物]] [[Category:Kamo clan]]
